# encodeurl

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Encode a URL to a percent-encoded form, excluding already-encoded sequences

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install encodeurl
```

## API

```js
var encodeUrl = require('encodeurl')
```

### encodeUrl(url)

Encode a URL to a percent-encoded form, excluding already-encoded sequences.

This function will take a URL and encode all characters that need to be encoded,
while preserving characters that are already encoded. This is useful when you want
to ensure a URL is fully encoded but don't want to double-encode any characters.

#### Arguments

- `url` - The URL string to encode.

#### Return value

A new string that is the `url` encoded in a percent-encoded form.

#### Example

```js
// Encodes a URL containing unencoded and encoded characters
encodeUrl('http://example.com/foo bar/baz')
// => 'http://example.com/foo%20bar/baz'

// Preserves already encoded characters
encodeUrl('http://example.com/foo%20bar/baz')
// => 'http://example.com/foo%20bar/baz'
```

## Purpose

This module is designed to be used in HTTP servers to encode a URL in a way that
is safe for use in HTTP headers, redirects, and other HTTP operations. It ensures
that URLs are properly encoded while avoiding double-encoding of already percent-encoded
sequences.

Common use cases include:

- Encoding URLs for HTTP Location headers in redirects
- Encoding URLs for HTML attributes
- Encoding URLs for API endpoints

## Comparison to other URL encoding functions

### `encodeUrl(url)` vs `encodeURI(url)`

The `encodeURI` function will not encode characters that have special meaning in a URL, such
as `/`, `?`, `#`, etc. The `encodeUrl` function will encode these characters if they appear
in parts of the URL where they would need to be encoded.

Additionally, `encodeUrl` will not double-encode already percent-encoded sequences, while
`encodeURI` might re-encode some percent-encoded sequences.

### `encodeUrl(url)` vs `encodeURIComponent(url)`

The `encodeURIComponent` function is designed to encode a component of a URI, not a complete
URI. It will encode more characters than necessary if used on a complete URL, including
characters that have special meaning in a URL like `/`, `:`, etc.
